_note: MOCK procedure result. No real patient. For PreChart development/eval.

procedure.name: Colonoscopy
procedure.indication: Positive fecal immunochemical test (FIT); diagnostic colonoscopy
procedure.extent_reached: Cecum (confirmed by appendiceal orifice and ileocecal valve)

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: Colonoscopy. INDICATION: positive screening FIT (2026-06-24) in a 65-year-old asymptomatic man; no anemia (Hgb 14.6). Low-dose aspirin continued periprocedurally per plan given remote bare-metal coronary stent (2014); no therapeutic anticoagulant on board. SEDATION: moderate. PREP: adequate (BBPS 8/9). FINDINGS: The colonoscope was advanced to the cecum and the terminal ileum was intubated and appeared normal. A 7 mm sessile polyp was identified in the ascending colon and removed by cold snare polypectomy; specimen retrieved and sent to pathology. A 4 mm sessile polyp in the descending colon was removed by cold snare polypectomy; specimen retrieved. Scattered non-bleeding diverticula were noted in the sigmoid colon. No mass, no ulceration, and no source of active bleeding was identified. Hemostasis was intact at both polypectomy sites; no bleeding despite continued aspirin. IMPRESSION: Two colon polyps removed (7 mm ascending, 4 mm descending); sigmoid diverticulosis. FIT positivity most likely attributable to the polyps. Pathology pending.
disposition: Discharged home same day in stable condition with escort. Post-polypectomy precautions reviewed. Low-dose aspirin continued without interruption; no bleeding at polypectomy sites.

followup.recommendation: Review polyp pathology (ascending 7 mm, descending 4 mm); call patient with results and set surveillance interval
Continue low-dose aspirin; no hold or bridging required (aspirin monotherapy, no therapeutic anticoagulant)

planted_followup_items.description: Polypectomy pathology pending for two specimens (7 mm ascending, 4 mm descending colon) — results must be communicated to the patient and drive the surveillance interval
planted_followup_items.expected_action: call patient with pathology results and document surveillance colonoscopy interval

planted_followup_items.description: Post-polypectomy surveillance colonoscopy interval to be set once histology returns (adenoma vs. serrated vs. hyperplastic determines 3-10 year interval)
planted_followup_items.expected_action: assign and document surveillance interval per pathology and polyp count/size

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored.

contacts.verbatim: My throat's a little scratchy and I've been pretty bloated and gassy since I got home. Is that normal?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat and colonic insufflation bloating
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat from the scope and gas/bloating from air insufflation are expected and self-limited over 24-48h. Walking and passing gas help.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-colonoscopy symptoms; no red flags, no escalation needed.

contacts.verbatim: When will I hear back about the polyps you took out? And should I keep taking my baby aspirin like normal?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending pathology timing and whether to continue aspirin
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: pathology expected back ~2026-08-08; the GI team will call with results and the surveillance interval. Confirm he should continue low-dose aspirin 81 mg without interruption — it was never held and no anticoagulant is involved.
contacts.rationale: Fully answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the pending_pathology item. Aspirin was continued throughout, so the correct guidance is simply to keep taking it — no resumption decision exists.

contacts.verbatim: I saw a tiny streak of pink on the toilet paper once after a bowel movement today. No pain, feel totally fine otherwise.
contacts.clinical_signal: Trivial self-limited spotting after cold-snare polypectomy; hemodynamically well, single minor episode, no clots or ongoing bleeding
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; a small amount of pink/light spotting after cold-snare polypectomy is common and benign. Advise return precautions: call or seek care for repeated or large-volume red blood, clots, black stools, lightheadedness, dizziness, or abdominal pain.
contacts.rationale: Triage trap (dangerous-sounding-but-benign): scant one-time spotting after low-risk cold-snare removal in an asymptomatic, hemodynamically stable patient is reassurable — not the delayed post-polypectomy hemorrhage pattern, which involves recurrent/large red blood or hemodynamic symptoms. Return precautions given so a true bleed would be caught.
